title: convert_str_or_uuid should try UUID conversion before returning str
body:
convert_str_or_uuid() in radicli/util.py currently always succeeds and returns a string or UUID, but if the input looks like a UUID (36 chars, 4 hyphens) and fails UUID validation (e.g. wrong checksum), it silently returns the malformed string instead of raising a helpful error.



Fix: if the value matches the UUID pattern (len==36, hyphens in right places) but fails UUID() parsing, raise ValueError with a clear message instead of falling back to str.
Add a test in radicli/tests/test_util.py for both valid UUIDs, plain strings, and malformed UUID-shaped strings.
